title: Methods for Selecting DOM Elements
description: A flowchart illustrating the primary JavaScript methods used to select elements from the Document Object Model (DOM), progressing from the most specific to the most general selectors.
elements: querySelector (Select first match of CSS selector)
querySelectorAll (Select all matches of CSS selector)
relationships: Starts from the Document Root, which branches to all selection methods. The methods are ordered by specificity, showing getElementById as the most precise, followed by querySelector, then getElementsByClassName, getElementsByTagName, and finally querySelectorAll as the most general and powerful.
